description:
Goal
Make provider-boundary Process.heap_alloc_stats/0 values flow through compiler2 without recursive overflow, so runtime refs and heap-stats fixtures compile normally through semantic closure and rooted quicksort fixtures that exercise the same provider path stop failing before transport/native lowering.

Minimal repros
- fn main() do
    stats = Process.heap_alloc_stats()
    dbg(stats)
  end
- fn main() do
    stats = Process.heap_alloc_stats()
    dbg(stats[:list_cons_allocs])
  end
- fixtures2/00007_runtime_refs.fz
- fixtures2/00297_heap_alloc_stats.fz
- fixtures2/00001_quicksort_plus_foo.fz via tests that root the full main/0 and therefore execute the same stats -> dbg / map-projection path

Observed facts
- Process.heap_alloc_stats() by itself compiles.
- dbg(%{list_cons_allocs: 1, map_allocs: 2}[:list_cons_allocs]) compiles.
- The overflow requires a value flowing from Process.heap_alloc_stats/0 into dbg or dbg after map projection from that provider result.
- LLDB traced the rooted quicksort overflow into src/compiler2/contract.rs:collect_ambiguous_empty_list_vars while semantic analysis applied function contracts to the provider-returned map path.
- The root bug was that the ambiguous-empty-list collector descended through concrete top types like any even though it only exists to find ambiguous type variables. any projects back to list(any), so the traversal re-entered itself forever.
- After the fix, the same fixtures advance past semantic analysis and any remaining failures belong to downstream native lowering in fz-hwn.10 rather than recursive overflow.

Why this matters
This is a separate provider-boundary / semantic-contract bug. It should not be “fixed” by widening callable-demand work or by reintroducing type-driven callable inventory. The right fix is to keep ambiguous-empty-list discovery scoped to polymorphic structure and terminate repeated pattern/witness traversals structurally.

Code seams to inspect
- src/modules/runtime_library/process.fz
- src/compiler2/contract.rs
- src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs
- src/compiler2/jobs/root.rs via fixtures2/00007_runtime_refs.fz and rooted quicksort fixtures
- src/compiler2/jobs/runtime_demand.rs provider-boundary return and structural reverse-propagation only if the semantic fix proves a downstream issue remains
- src/compiler2/jobs/artifact.rs runtime param / ABI derivation for provider-returned values only after semantic closure is sound

Acceptance signal
- compiler2_runtime_refs_pull_only_the_reached_runtime_modules resolves without ignore
- minimal repros above no longer stack-overflow in semantic analysis
- rooted quicksort proof tests that use fixtures2/00001_quicksort_plus_foo.fz advance past semantic analysis; any later LowerNativeProgram failures are owned by fz-hwn.10
- no new callable-entry or callable-boundary inventory appears for these value-only paths
title: Compiler2 runtime refs: Process.heap_alloc_stats dbg path stack-overflows during compilation
